name: Paragon Bank
description_short: Community bank founded in 2005 offering personal and business banking, mortgages, and online/mobile solutions with a focus on local decision-making and customer relationships.
description_long:
Paragon Bank was founded in January 2005 and received its bank charter that same month, later listing on the Nasdaq Bulletin Board in September 2005. The bank was established on three core values: Integrity, Dedication, and Knowledge. Over its 20-year history, Paragon has positioned itself as a community-focused financial institution that emphasizes relationship banking and local market expertise.

Paragon offers a comprehensive range of banking services including personal banking, business banking, and mortgage lending. Customers can access online and mobile banking platforms with features such as mobile check deposits, bill pay, instant transfers, account alerts, and statements. The bank provides traditional banking products and services designed to serve both individual consumers and business clients, with the ability to make mortgage payments through their online system.

Paragon distinguishes itself through its community bank model, emphasizing that decisions are made locally and quickly rather than through distant corporate hierarchies. The bank offers custom financial solutions tailored to individual needs and actively invests in community engagement through its ACTS Community Engagement program, which provides each team member 40 paid volunteer hours annually focused on education, hunger, and shelter/housing initiatives. The bank's "My Paragon" program features customer success stories designed to build trust through real customer experiences rather than scripted marketing.

Paragon Bank operates as a traditional community bank with full-service offerings. While the website demonstrates commitment to technology and community values, specific details about product rates, fees, account minimums, and competitive advantages compared to other banks are not disclosed on the available web pages. For consumers seeking a community banking relationship with local decision-making, Paragon appears positioned as an alternative to larger national banks, though pricing and detailed product comparisons would require direct contact. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Personal banking accounts and deposit products
Mortgage lending and mortgage payment processing
Online banking platform with login and account access
Bill pay and instant money transfers
Account alerts and fraud prevention monitoring
Customer relationship management and personalized service
pros: Offers full-service banking including personal, business, and mortgage products in one institution
Mobile check deposit and bill pay features for convenient banking on-the-go
Local decision-making and quick approvals as a community bank rather than corporate entity
40 paid volunteer hours per year for employees focused on community education, hunger, and housing programs
20+ years of established banking operations since 2005 with consistent charter and regulatory status
Online and mobile banking with account alerts to help prevent fraud
Customized financial solutions tailored to individual customer needs
cons: Website does not disclose specific interest rates, APYs, or fee structures for any products
No information about account minimums, deposit insurance details, or product-specific terms
Limited geographic presence implied (community bank model) with no service area or branch locations clearly stated on home page
No mention of specialized services like investment management, wealth planning, or business lines of credit
Mortgage payment portal requires sign-in, suggesting limited information availability to non-customers
best_for: Consumers seeking traditional community banking with local relationship-based service
Small business owners needing personalized banking solutions and quick local decisions
Customers interested in banking with institutions actively engaged in community volunteering and social responsibility
Mortgage borrowers preferring local lender relationships over large national mortgage servicers
diagnosis: Paragon Bank is best for consumers and small business owners seeking a traditional community banking relationship with local decision-making and personalized service, particularly those who value community engagement. The primary caveat is that detailed product rates, fees, and account terms are not disclosed on their website, requiring direct contact to compare competitiveness against other banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
